tournaments.raw_address: The Ex Servo\'s  157 Junction St   Nowra NSW 2541
tournaments.details:
Shoalhaven monthly IFPA Comp. 
8 rounds of 2 x 4 player games.
7, 5, 3, 1 Points

If over 16 players top 8 finals.
If over 16 players top 8 finals.
2 semi finals x 2 4 players games . Top two from each semi advance to final which is also 2 x 4 player games 

$21 entry.  $10 each entrant contributes towards prize money plus trophy for the winner care of AMD.

32 player maximum, places can be reserved to ensure entry..

Tournament Date: Starting January 7th, 2026 and 1st Wednesday of each month

Tournament will be Group Matchplay
Qualifying - 3 Rounds of 2 matches (6 qualifying games)
Balanced pairing, Scoring system: IFPA (7/5/3/1) for 4player groups (7/4/1) for 3 player groups.

Arena draws: Attempts will be made to assign an arena that hasn't been played the fewest times by all opponents.
Three rounds of two games per round.
Tiebreaker: Ties will not be broken and number of 1st then 2nds will be used to separate any ties on points

The finals will consist of the top 8 (or top 4 field if less than 16players).
Semi Final - players will be seeded into two groups using Matchplay. Each group will play 1 semi final with placings awarded 7/5/3/1 with the top 2 players from each group going through to the final
Final - The top four players will play 1x final


ENTRY FEE:
$6 ($5 into the prize pool, the $1 IFPA\Aus Champ fee)
Pay per Play - Games are $2 or $5 for 3 games
Expect to play 7 or 8 games if you make to the final 4
Prizes 1st, 2nd, 3rd, will receive cash from the $5 entrance fee & 4th will receive their $5 back
There will also be random prizes on the night donated by the Lost Souls & AMD (if available)

tournaments.raw_address: Illawarra Clubhouse  147 Kembla St  Wollongong NSW 2500
tournaments.details:
Qualifying Rounds
Format: Qualifying will consist of 8 rounds of group matchplay, with each round featuring 2 games, totaling 16 games.
Group Structure: Players will compete in 4-player or 3-player groups.
4-Player Groups: IFPA scoring (7/5/3/1).
3-Player Groups: IFPA scoring (7/4/1).
Pairing & Arena Assignment: Balanced pairing, random playing order, and balanced arena assignment will be used.

Tiebreakers

Key Situations:
For critical scenarios such as earning a bye, advancing to the finals, or progressing within the finals, tiebreakers will be resolved using a randomly selected arena via Matchplay. This can include banned games.

All Other Situations:
Ties for seeding and less critical places will be resolved automatically, based on the number of 1st and 2nd place finishes.
Example: If a machine is banned in the finals and 3rd and 4th seeds in the group are tied, the pick goes to the player with the greater number of 1st-place finishes, followed by 2nd-place finishes.

Finals Structure
Player Count Over 32
Top 16:

Player Count Over 24:
Top 12 Players Advance: The top 4 receive a bye in the first round.

Top 12 Finals Breakdown:

Quarterfinals:
Group Q1: Seeds 5, 8, 9, and 12.
Group Q2: Seeds 6, 7, 10, and 11.
Top 2 players from each group advance to the semifinals.

Semifinals:
Group S1: Seeds 1, 4, and top 2 qualifiers from QF
Group S2: Seeds 2, 3, and top 2 qualifiers from QF
The top 2 players from each group advance to the finals.

Player Count 24 or Fewer:
Top 8 Players Advance: No byes are given.

Top 8 Finals Breakdown:

Semifinals:
Group S1: Seeds 1, 4, 5, and 8.
Group S2: Seeds 2, 3, 6, and 7.
The top 2 players from each group advance to the finals.

Finals:
The top 2 players from each semifinal group compete in the final round.

Game Selection & Banning

Quarterfinals:
Top 2 seeds in each group may select games but cannot ban games.

Semifinals:
Top 2 seeds in each group may choose or ban games. A maximum of 2 games can be banned per group.
Once a game is selected or banned, it is unavailable for the remainder of the competition/
If a machine is banned, the next highest seed in the group gains the choice.

Sudden-Death Tiebreakers
In the event of a tie during finals progression (e.g., advancing to the next round or determining the winner), a sudden-death match will be played on a randomly selected machine.
Software: The finals will be managed using Matchplay software.

Final Standings:
Final standings are determined by the round in which a player is eliminated.

Players eliminated in the same round are ranked against one another based on their performance in that specific round, using Matchplay points.

Scores are not cumulative across multiple rounds of the finals. Each round is treated independently.
Example: If two players are eliminated in the semifinals, their ranking will be based on the Matchplay points earned during the semifinal round only.

For players who advance to the final round, their standings will be based on their Matchplay scores in the finals
If there is a tie in the finals e.g. for podium finishes see key situation tiebreaker rules.

tournaments.raw_address: Bayside Anglican Church Hall  Pitt-Owen Street, Arncliffe, NSW
tournaments.details:
Thursday Max Matchplay

5:45pm - Rego & Free play

6:30pm - Comp starts.

$25 Entry - Cash

3 hours of single game head to head play. Scoring is +1/0

Top 4 proceed to finals playing 4 machines concurrently. Play 1 ball, move to the enxt machine, play one ball etc until completed. PAPA scoring 4/2/1/0

Machines randomly chosen by Matchplay software

tournaments.raw_address: Bayside Anglican Church Hall  Pitt-Owen Street, Arncliffe
tournaments.details:
Flipper Fellowship Saturday Group Match Play

$30 Entry - Cash only

Free play: 9:00am - 9:45am

Comp: 10:00am

Qualifying: 7/8 rounds x 2 games.  4  or 3 player pairings.  7-5-3-1 / 7-4-1 scoring using Matchplay software.

Finals:  7-5-3-1 scoring
-	Semi Final. Top 8 qualifiers.  1 round x 2 games.  Top 2 qualifiers in each group will have choice of machine.  Top 2 in each group will progress.
-	Final.  Remaining 4 players will compete in 1 round x 2 games.  Top 2 qualifiers will have choice of machine.

Prizes: Top 4 Trophies & cash. Lucky door & last place prize may also be awarded.

Tiebreakers:  Critical tiebreakers (byes, finals positions etc) will be determined by a single elimination game on a random machine.  Positional tiebreakers determined by Matchplay software.  Machine selection determined by highest number of 1st, 2nd, 3rd.

If the 7th round finishes by 3:45pm, we will commence an 8th round, pending other matters and at TD's discretion.

Semi-Final / Final:  Number of players may be amended pending number of qualifiers.

Thursday Group Matchplay

5:45pm – Rego & free play.

6:30pm - Comp begins.

7/5/3/1 - 7/4/1 scoring

Qualifying: 4 rounds x 2 games / round

Semi-final: Top 8 (or top 4 if less than 16 players). 1 game semi final. Top 2 players from each group proceed to the Final. Machines randomly selected by Matchplay

tournaments.raw_address: Tattersalls Hotel  Station St & Union Ln,  Penrith NSW 2750
tournaments.details:
Tournament will be Group Matchplay
Qualifying - 5 Rounds
Round pairing: Players will be randomly paired against each other in all rounds.
Scoring system: IFPA (7/5/3/1) for 4player grps (7/4/1) for 3 player grps.
Arena draws: Attempts will be made to assign an arena that has been played the fewest times by all opponents.
Five rounds of one game per round.
Tiebreaker: Ties for final will be a sudden death game
 
The finals will consist of the top 8 (or top 4 field if less than 16players).
Semi Final - players will be seeded into two groups with the top two players from each group going through to the final. Ea group will play 1 elimination final with placings awarded 7/5/3/1 with the top 2 players from ea group going through to the final (Semis may change to 3 game chaos format to max. pts if we can get a sixth pin on site)
Final - The top four players will play 1 sudden death final
 
ENTRY FEE: $6 ($5 into the prize pool, the $1 IFPA\Aus Champ fee)
Newer Games are $2 & Older Games are $1 ea  (this is public venue)
expect to play 7 games if you make to the final 4

tournaments.raw_address: Bayside Anglican Church Hall  Pitt-Owen Street, Arncliffe, NSW
tournaments.details:
10:00am Start

Flipper Fellowship will play host to the top 24 players from the NSW 2025 rankings to battle it out in a best of 7 single elimination tournament to crown the NSW State Champion and win great prizes.

Top 6 finalists will receive automatic qualification for the 25/26 IFPA Australian Pinball Championship to be held in Wollongong.

This event will be invitation only.  Current standings available here http://ifpapinball.com/series/acs/2025/standingsView.php?l=NSW
